title: Shift-aware weighting plus harsher grouped/temporal validation
summary: The local-public gap suggests some fold optimism.
rationale: The local-public gap suggests some fold optimism. With 2022-2025 data and test-only drivers/races, this is a covariate-shift problem; standard grouped CV can still overrate features that do not transfer well to the held-out season/race mix.
implementation_hint: Run adversarial validation to predict train vs test using only features available at inference, then use that score for sample weighting or for selecting a ‘stable’ feature subset. Evaluate models under a stricter scheme such as `StratifiedGroupKFold` on `(Year,Race)` plus a forward-style split by year/race order, and tune blends for worst-fold or mean-minus-std AUC rather than mean AUC alone.
expected_effect: May not raise raw CV, but should reduce public-LB decay and favor features that transfer to unseen races/drivers.
risk: Importance weighting can be high-variance when estimated badly; stricter CV can make iteration slower and may underfit if over-penalized.
